evals.name: staged precommit full prepush
evals.prompt: A user asks: set up git hooks with gitleaks, linting on commit, and full tests before push. Describe the plan before editing files.
evals.expected_output: The response inspects existing hooks/config first, proposes fast staged pre-commit checks with Gitleaks, full pre-push validation, and avoids overwriting hooks without approval.
assertions: The output checks existing hook configuration before changing it.
The output keeps pre-commit fast and scoped to staged or affected files.
The output runs or proposes Gitleaks secret scanning with redacted output.
The output reserves full build/test/lint validation for pre-push.
The output asks before overwriting hooks or changing git config.

evals.name: gitignore tracked file safety
evals.prompt: A user says: add build artifacts to .gitignore and stop tracking the ones already committed. Describe the safe workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response derives ignore rules from actual artifacts, checks whether files are tracked or ignored, updates .gitignore narrowly, and asks before git rm --cached.
assertions: The output inspects actual generated artifacts before adding ignore rules.
The output uses git check-ignore or git ls-files to verify behavior.
The output avoids broad ignore patterns that hide source files.
The output asks before removing tracked files from the index.
The output does not delete the user's working files.
